c1.speaker: narrator
c1.line: By the old stone table in the garden, she talks without being asked.

c2.speaker: clara
c2.line: Father used to give us puzzles. Every birthday, one puzzle, one box, two children. And they were designed, Mr. Gibbons — designed so neither of us could solve them alone. Julian would find the mad sideways idea; I'd find the flaw in it; between us, the box opened. Every single time.

c3.speaker: clara
c3.line: I used to think it was a game. Lately I think it was a syllabus.
